description: Tell definitions for scripts/deslop-lint.py. Each tell is matched against a line after fenced code, inline code, and front matter are stripped. Order matters: tells run top to bottom, and 'pre-entity' tells run before HTML entities are masked. See conventions/writing-style.md for the rules behind each one.

tells.name: em-dash
tells.message: em dash as an HTML entity, write it out plainly

tells.name: section-sign
tells.message: section sign as an HTML entity, write 'section'

tells.name: middle-dot
tells.message: middle dot or bullet as an HTML entity, join the items with a comma or plain words

tells.name: em-dash
tells.message: em dash in prose, use a colon, comma, parentheses, or two sentences

tells.name: middle-dot
tells.message: middle dot or bullet in prose, join the items with a comma or plain words

tells.name: bold-bullet-lead
tells.message: bolded bullet lead, reserve bold for a rare callout not a per-item label

tells.name: id-label-lead
tells.message: id label on a list item, number the list plainly

tells.name: semicolon
tells.message: semicolon in prose, prefer a full stop

tells.name: not-just-x-but-y
tells.message: "not just X but Y" padding, make the point once

tells.name: filler-verb
tells.message: filler verb, use a plain verb or cut

tells.name: marketing-adjective
tells.message: marketing adjective, say what is true

tells.name: throat-clearing
tells.pattern: ^\s*(?:[-*+]\s+|>\s*|#{1,6}\s+)?(In today's|It's worth noting that|When it comes to|At its core|Furthermore|Moreover|Additionally|That being said)\b
tells.message: throat-clearing or transition opener, start with the point

tells.name: vague-intensifier
tells.message: vague intensifier with no number behind it

tells.name: emoji
tells.message: emoji or decorative checkmark in body text
